name: The stone which the builders rejected
notes: Psalm 118:22-23 hails a rejected stone made the chief cornerstone, the LORD's marvellous doing. It is among the most-quoted Old Testament texts in the New, applied to Christ Himself: by the Lord in the three Synoptic accounts of the vineyard parable (Matt 21:42, Mark 12:10-11, Luke 20:17), by Peter before the council (Acts 4:11), and again in 1 Peter 2:7. The shared core is the builders' rejection of the stone that becomes the head of the corner. The citing verbs vary as complementary vantages on the one act: refused (Ps), rejected (Synoptics), set at nought (Acts), disallowed (1 Pet). The Lord's-doing acclamation of v23 is carried over in Matthew and Mark, while Luke, Acts, and 1 Peter draw out the cornerstone clause alone.
